title: LITMUS∞: SMT-Verified Memory Model Portability Checking for Cross-Architecture Concurrent Code

reviewers.name: Yuan Cheng
reviewers.key_strength: Cross-solver validation uses Wilson confidence intervals correctly, and the two-configuration Z3 + independent CVC5 validation provides meaningful statistical rigor uncommon in verification tools.
reviewers.key_weakness: The LLM OOD evaluation set contains only ~15 samples, yielding a 95% Wilson CI of [68.1%, 99.8%]—far too wide to support the headline 93.3% accuracy claim.

reviewers.name: Aniruddha Sinha
reviewers.key_strength: The certificate infrastructure with 1,400 SMT certificates, Alethe proof export, and cross-solver validation follows best practices for TCB minimization in verification tools.
reviewers.key_weakness: The 140-pattern ceiling fundamentally limits the tool to known bugs; systematic state-space exploration via bounded model checking would address the false-negative gap for novel concurrency violations.

reviewers.name: Lila Zhang
reviewers.key_strength: The architecture correctly maintains SMT certification as the soundness backbone, and the custom memory model DSL provides a principled knowledge representation with 99.4% empirical correspondence to herd7 .cat specifications.
reviewers.key_weakness: The single DSL-to-.cat disagreement (170/171) is insufficiently characterized—it could indicate a semantics mismatch that propagates to incorrect verdicts for programs exercising the mismodeled behavior.

reviewers.name: Sara Roy
reviewers.key_strength: The hybrid LLM-SMT architecture where LLM affects recall only while all verdicts remain SMT-certified is the gold standard for integrating ML into safety-critical verification pipelines.
reviewers.key_weakness: The ML evaluation uses GPT-4.1 for headline accuracy but defaults to GPT-4.1-nano in practice, with no accuracy reported for the deployed model, and the 203-sample benchmark lacks per-category stratification.

reviewers.name: Joseph S. Chang
reviewers.key_strength: The comprehensive certificate infrastructure with 993 Alethe UNSAT proofs and 407 self-certifying SAT witnesses, combined with cross-solver validation, follows best practices in certified automated reasoning.
reviewers.key_weakness: The system applies well-understood SMT techniques without advancing automated reasoning itself—no new decision procedures, proof strategies, or theory combinations are introduced.

consensus_strengths: The SMT certification pipeline is comprehensive and follows best practices: 1,400 certificates with Alethe proofs, cross-solver Z3/CVC5 validation, and SMT-LIB2 export for independent third-party verification.

The hybrid LLM-SMT architecture correctly separates recall (ML-assisted pattern recognition) from soundness (SMT-certified verdicts), providing a clean and principled ML-verification integration model.
Practical engineering quality is excellent: sub-second full analysis, pip-installable CLI with JSON output, GitHub Actions workflow, and actionable fence recommendations for CI integration.
Honest scoping and limitation disclosure—including the 140-pattern ceiling, analytical fence costs, GPU model validation limitations, and Lean proof sketch status—reflects scientific maturity.
consensus_weaknesses: The LLM OOD evaluation is statistically underpowered (~15 samples), the headline GPT-4.1 accuracy does not match the deployed GPT-4.1-nano default, and the AST confidence fallback threshold is undisclosed and uncalibrated.
The 140-pattern library's coverage of real-world concurrency bugs is characterized only at an aggregate level (85.4%); no per-category, per-language, or usage-weighted coverage analysis is provided.
The single DSL-to-.cat disagreement (170/171) is identified but not formally resolved—it could indicate a semantic modeling error affecting verdicts for programs exercising asymmetric fence behavior.
Compositionality guarantees ('exact for disjoint vars, conservative for shared vars') lack formal proof; the Lean 4 proof sketches contain sorry'd subgoals, and the conservatism is unquantified.

The system applies established SMT techniques to a well-studied domain without introducing new decision procedures, proof strategies, or theory combinations—the contribution is engineering rather than theoretical.
actionable_recommendations: Expand the LLM OOD evaluation to at least 100 diverse samples with per-category breakdowns, report accuracy for both GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1-nano, and disclose the AST confidence fallback threshold with its precision-recall tradeoff.
Integrate an independent Alethe proof checker (e.g., carcara) into the CI pipeline to verify all 993 UNSAT proofs end-to-end, reducing Z3's role in the trusted computing base.
Formally resolve the single DSL-to-.cat disagreement by extending DSL expressiveness, proving conservatism of the divergence, or precisely documenting which programs are affected.

Mechanize the compositionality proof in Lean 4 (completing the sorry'd subgoals), or at minimum provide a formal statement of the conservatism guarantee with empirical false-positive rate measurements on real programs.
Add bounded model checking capabilities for small code fragments, leveraging the existing SMT encoding infrastructure to move beyond fixed-pattern matching and discover novel concurrency violations.
